scenario: difficulty finding purpose and meaning after retirement

turns.user_message: I retired six months ago and I thought I'd love it but I feel useless. Forty years of working and now I don't know what I'm for.
I appreciate that. It's just hard to put into words.
It's been going on for a while, honestly. I just kept pushing it aside.
Yeah. And what makes it harder is that nobody around me seems to understand.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with retirement is never going to get better.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about retirement and I should just deal with it.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by retirement more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of retirement because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting retirement control my life more than I realized.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about retirement and I should just deal with it.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about retirement and I should just deal with it.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I think the real issue isn't retirement — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
I think the real issue isn't retirement — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
I just realized something. the retirement is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
I think the real issue isn't retirement — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
I think what I'm taking away from today is I'm allowed to struggle with this and that doesn't make me weak. I want to sit with that.
I think what I'm taking away from today is I'm allowed to struggle with this and that doesn't make me weak. I want to sit with that.
